channel.title: math.GN updates on arXiv.org
channel.description: math.GN updates on the arXiv.org e-print archive.

item.title: Normality in the square of the Sorgenfrey Line
item.description: arXiv:2511.12327v2 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We consider sets of reals $X$ endowed with the Sorgenfrey lower limit topology denoted $X[\leq]$. Przymusi\'nski proved that if $X$ is a $Q$-set then $(X[\leq])^2$ is normal. While the converse is not in general true we consider examples of sets of the reals for which $(X[\leq])^2$ is normal or just pseudo-normal. For example, if $X$ is a $\lambda$ set, then $(X[\leq])^2$ is pseudo-normal but assuming CH there is an $X$ concentrated on a countable dense subset (so not a $\lambda$-set) but still $(X[\leq])^2$ is normal.
item.dc:creator: Paul Szeptycki, Hongwei Wen

item.title: The Extended Real Line with Reentry: Separating US from KC in the Clontz Hierarchy
item.description: arXiv:2603.03228v4 Announce Type: replace Abstract: We construct the Extended Real Line with Reentry (ERI), a quotient of $\overline{\mathbb{R}} = [-\infty,+\infty]$ that is compact, path-connected, sober, US (uniquely sequential), and not KC (kompacts closed)--the first compact path-connected space known to separate US from KC (see Remark 1.3 for a systematic verification against the pi-Base database [17]). In the Clontz hierarchy [2], ERI is $k_2$-Hausdorff but not weakly Hausdorff. The construction generalizes to a Filter-Modified Quotient (FMQ) framework that produces US-not-KC spaces from any compact Hausdorff base without isolated points. A modifier spectrum identifies an optimal "US zone," and an extended rigidity analysis shows that the hierarchy level $k_2$H-not-wH is preserved under arbitrary closed nowhere-dense collapse sets, iterated FMQ, and products--with first-countability of the base as the precise frontier for potential new levels.
item.dc:creator: Damian Rafael Lattenero
